title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, and Ambiguity · Execution Trace: Publisher Ledger Cleanup
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must enable confirms before publishing, drain confirmation channels, bound outstanding sequence numbers, and reconcile close ambiguity; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the publisher ledger cleanup increment in a bounded Go confirm-window ledger. Learners prog…
objectives: Retire confirmed sequence entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove no ledger or goroutine leak across generations.

Classify unresolved confirms after failure as ambiguous, retain stable message identity, and republish only through a duplicate-safe downstream contract; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
